ideal_must_detect.matched_text: proposed acquisition of BlueCedar Robotics Pte. Ltd

PDPA assessment: the NRIC is personal data under PDPA; however, the incident involves a single individual with low risk of significant harm, and does not meet notifiable data breach thresholds; the affected individual will be notified by HR.

MNPI/SFA/SGX assessment: accompanying materials included a draft integration timetable for the proposed acquisition of BlueCedar Robotics Pte. Ltd. (UEN: 201834567Q) and an employee transfer FAQ; these items track the issuer’s SGXNet announcement dated 15 May and do not introduce non‑generally available information; no SFA s.218/219 concerns identified.

Next steps: run a joint tabletop with vendor against MAS cyber hygiene guidance (non‑FI applicability), refresh staff phishing simulations, and confirm closure with an attestation from Islet Registry Services.

Contacts (role-only): dpo@lumisands.com.sg; ir@lumisands.com.sg; legal-ops@harboursquarelaw.sg for query consolidation; no personal emails should be used for incident correspondence.
Registered office (issuer): 12 Harbour Crescent #15-01, Singapore 000000; any mailed notices should reference “Incident Ref: LSR-OPS-2026-05-28-A”
ideal_must_detect.reason: moved from must_detect after strict runtime no longer emitted exact span during Stage B baseline reconciliation

ideal_must_detect.matched_text:
hr-queries@lumisands.com.sg), leading to brief unauthorised access to a cloud folder titled “FORM: Onboarding — Contractors”.
Data believed accessed: one contractor’s NRIC S9812043I contained in a scanned right-to-work checklist; other visible fields were generic placeholders (e.g., “Mobile: +65 6XXX XXXX”, “Bank A/c: 0XX-XXXX-XX”, “Address: [Residential Address]”).
Files in the same folder included blank templates (“FORM: Employee Banking Details (Sample)”), a role directory, and a public-helpline sheet (“IT Support: 1800-555-2828”), with no full personal emails or direct phone numbers disclosed.
Vendor context: the folder was synced by Islet Registry Services Pte. Ltd. (UEN: 201912345X) under a payroll migration work order; their incident ticket references “IRS-INC-000742” and a public helpdesk line 1800-555-9090.
PDPA assessment: the NRIC is personal data under PDPA; however, the incident involves a single individual with low risk of significant harm, and does not meet notifiable data breach thresholds; the affected individual will be notified by HR.
MNPI/SFA/SGX assessment: accompanying materials included a draft integration timetable for the proposed acquisition of BlueCedar Robotics Pte. Ltd. (UEN: 201834567Q) and an employee transfer FAQ; these items track the issuer’s SGXNet announcement dated 15 May and do not introduce non‑generally available information; no SFA s.218/219 concerns identified.
Listing Manual Rule 703: no further disclosure obligation arises at this time; the issuer’s Catalist sponsor/Company Secretary will keep SGX RegCo apprised if facts change; public enquiries may be routed to SGX Issuer Services (public helpline) 1800-555-2020.
Containment: HR mailbox password rotation completed; OAuth tokens revoked; vendor sync keys reset; audit logs retained; no evidence of lateral movement to capital-markets or M&A repositories.
Employment/privacy follow‑up: stale copies of “FORM: NRIC/FIN” and “FORM: Emergency Contact” templates removed from shared folders; data minimisation and retention settings updated per internal PDPA policy.
Contacts (role-only): dpo@lumisands.com.sg; ir@lumisands.com.sg; legal-ops@harboursquarelaw.sg for query consolidation; no personal emails should be used for incident correspondence.
Registered office (issuer): 12 Harbour Crescent #15-01, Singapore 000000; any mailed notices should reference “Incident Ref: LSR-OPS-2026-05-28-A”
